summary: FAIL — Two findings escalated by Ingi: (1) bare catch{} must never silently swallow errors, developer must be notified; (2) hiding the real error message from users in TryResolvePath is unacceptable. Both need coder fixes before merge.

previous_reviews_assessed.codeanalyzer: partial — said 'catch filters correct' in v2 but missed the bare catch{} at FluidProvider.cs:137. All other findings were thorough.
previous_reviews_assessed.tester: agree — correctly identified false-green callGoal tests in v1, verified fixes in v2. Minor findings are correctly classified.
previous_reviews_assessed.security: agree — SSTI as accepted-risk is the right call. ValidatePath sandbox is effective. Resource exhaustion findings are appropriately low severity.

findings.issue: Bare catch{} in RegisterTypeIfNeeded silently swallows ALL exceptions. Ingi: bare catch{} is never allowed — developer must be notified when something goes wrong. Find and fix ALL bare catch{} blocks in the codebase, not just this one.
findings.impact: Masks bugs during development. If MemberAccessStrategy.Register throws due to a Fluid bug, no one knows. Silent error swallowing is always critical.
findings.suggestion: At minimum: log/surface the error. Add a catch filter to let fatal exceptions propagate. Scan the entire codebase for bare catch{} and fix them all.

findings.issue: callGoal success tests (lines 231, 479, 502, 534) only assert DoesNotContain('[Error:') — they never verify the actual output value. The GetNumber test sets value=42 but never checks '42' appears in output.
findings.impact: A bug in callGoal value serialization would pass all tests.

findings.issue: TryResolvePath hides the real error from ValidatePath. When it throws, the exception propagates through Fluid and gets caught with a generic 'Template render error' message. The user never sees what actually went wrong. Ingi: hiding the real error message is really bad.
findings.impact: User gets an unhelpful generic error instead of the actual path validation failure. Makes debugging template includes much harder.
findings.suggestion: TryResolvePath should catch ValidatePath exceptions and return null (so GetFileInfo falls through to NotFoundFileInfo). The real error is 'file not found at this path', not 'template render error'.

findings.issue: using System.IO — technically against 'NEVER use System.IO' rule but only used for StringWriter, MemoryStream, and IOException (in-memory types), not filesystem operations.
findings.impact: None — false positive on the rule.

findings.suggestion: No action needed unless Fluid adds caching.
